sourceMovie.title: Warm Bodies
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Warm Bodies for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jonathan Levine keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shaun of the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Warm Bodies clicked for you, Shaun of the Dead (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Clueless
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Warm Bodies clicked for you, Clueless (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Warm Bodies?
faq.answer: Try Shaun of the Dead, Clueless, She's the Man—each shares DNA with Warm Bodies while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Warm Bodies?
faq.answer: Warm Bodies (2013) is best described as Horror, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Warm Bodies worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
